## ⚠️ Data & pipeline notice (read first)

This run hit a **pipeline gap** that shapes everything below:

- **No EOD wrap ran on 18 May, and no run of any kind on 19 May.** As a result there was **no opening-bias forecast generated for today (20 May)** — the forecast pipeline stalled at the 18 May morning brief.
- **`market-prices.json` was last refreshed 19 May 23:46 IST** and holds the **19 May close**, not today's NSE open. No live GIFT Nifty pre-open, US futures, or fresh wire news were available to this run.
- **Repairs made this run:** I backfilled 18 May's actual close (₹23,649.95) and scored its previously-PENDING session direction, and created today's session entry with a fresh direction forecast. Today's **opening bias is marked NOT SCORABLE** — see below.

Everything that follows leans on the 19 May close snapshot plus the documented market regime. Treat the intraday levels as reference, not live ticks.

## 1. Latest market snapshot (19 May close)

| Index | Close | Day Δ% | Prev close |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Nifty 50** | 23,618.00 | −0.14% | 23,649.95 |
| **Sensex** | 75,200.85 | −0.15% | 75,315.04 |
| **Bank Nifty** | 53,409.15 | −0.24% | 53,537.00 |

A quiet, mildly negative consolidation session. The bigger story is what came *before* it: on 18 May the Nifty gapped down hard (−1.22%, open 23,354) on the US–Iran peace failure and Brent spike, then **recovered the entire gap to close 23,649.95** (+0.03% on the day). 19 May simply drifted −0.14% off that recovered level. Net: two sessions, almost no net change — the index is sitting mid-range, neither broken nor breaking out.

**Commodities / macro (overnight, 19 May):**

- **Brent** $110.37 (−1.54%) — still elevated, fragile-peace premium intact.
- **WTI** $103.27 (−4.96%) — sharp drop; wide ~$7 Brent–WTI spread.
- **Gold** $4,503.6 (−1.07%), **Silver** $74.83 (−2.91%) — havens softening overnight.
- **USDINR** ≈ 96 — near record-weak; ongoing rupee-depreciation stress.

The overnight tape leans risk-on at the margin (oil, gold, silver all lower), which slightly eases the macro headwind into today.

## 2. Opening bias verdict — NOT SCORABLE

**Forecast → Actual = NOT SCORABLE.**

There was no opening-bias forecast to grade for 20 May (the EOD runs that generate it didn't fire on 18–19 May), and today's actual NSE open isn't present in the data (latest = 19 May close). I will not fabricate an "actual open." The session entry records this as `NOT_SCORABLE` with `validation_required: true` so a later run can revisit if today's open data arrives.

**Catch-up scoring (18 May, done this run):** Forecast was `BEARISH_WITH_SUPPORT_BOUNCE_ATTEMPT` (−0.9%, close 23,250–23,500). Actual close 23,649.95 — a full intraday recovery, **above** the forecast range. **Grade: WRONG.** It matched the low-probability (10%) `IRAN_HEADLINE_REVERSAL` scenario: the DII floor and dip-buying absorbed the gap-down rather than sellers pressing at 23,500. Lesson logged: a bearish session call into a record-oversold geopolitical gap, with an intact DII floor, repeatedly underestimates intraday short-covering.

## 3. Today's session-direction forecast

> **Direction: FLAT → SIDEWAYS with a mild negative tilt**
> **Conviction: 2 / 5** *(fragile regime, no fresh catalyst, but live binary Iran headline risk)*
> **Reference close: 23,618 · Expected range: 23,450 – 23,720 · Central: ≈ −0.2%**

**Key assumption:** The index recovered the Iran gap and is consolidating, not trending. Brent at $110 remains a macro headwind, but overnight commodity softening (gold −1.1%, silver −2.9%, WTI −5.0%) trims the risk-off pressure. DII floor intact, FII selling decelerating. With no fresh Iran catalyst and pre-RBI (23 May) positioning caution, base case is range-bound trade with a slight downward lean. Resistance 23,800 caps; support 23,500, then the 23,356 / 23,178 cluster.

**Scenarios:**

| Scenario | Prob | Nifty close | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Range-bound flat** | 45% | 23,550–23,700 | No catalyst; sector rotation, DII supports. −0.2% to +0.3% |
| **Negative drift** | 30% | 23,400–23,550 | Brent $110+, rupee 96, pre-RBI caution, residual FII selling. −0.3% to −0.9% |
| **Relief bounce** | 15% | 23,720–23,850 | Commodity softening extends + Iran de-escalation headline → short-covering. +0.4% to +1.0% |
| **Brent-shock down** | 10% | 23,250–23,400 | Brent breaks $113 / Hormuz incident → tests 23,356. −1.0% to −1.6%, high-VIX |

**Brent forecast:** $108–112 working range; floor ~$105 absent explicit de-escalation; Iran nuclear-proposal binary still unresolved.

## 7. Top risks & opportunities

**Risks**
1. **Iran nuclear-proposal binary** — Trump's 16 May proposal is still unanswered. An Iranian rejection or an Israel–Iran kinetic event spikes Brent >$113 and reopens the 18 May down-shock playbook.
2. **Brent stickiness at $110 + USDINR ~96** — twin macro headwinds for OMCs (MRPL), aviation (IndiGo) and import-cost inflation; caps any sustained Nifty push toward 23,800.
3. **Pre-RBI (23 May) positioning** — rate-direction uncertainty keeps Bank Nifty / NBFCs choppy and can sap conviction this week.

**Opportunities**
1. **Overnight commodity softening** — gold, silver and WTI all lower; if it extends, the haven unwind plus a calmer Brent could fuel the 15% relief-bounce scenario toward 23,800.
2. **Defense-premium persistence** — Astra Micro and the broader defense complex remain the portfolio's resilient leg (M012 v5).
3. **DII floor reliability** — the 18 May full-gap recovery reconfirmed strong domestic dip-buying around 23,350–23,500; constructive for buy-the-dip setups while support holds.
